title: pelargonium × puniceum
intro: Pelargonium × punicum, also known as the African geranium, is a hybrid species of the pelargonium genus, characterized by its fragrant flowers and medicinal properties. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for their antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which have been found to be beneficial in treating various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of African geranium include its ability to soothe coughs, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy in many cultures. African geranium can be prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
